body: The market capitalization of the Indonesia Stock Exchange increased by 1.15% month-to-month in October 2024, reaching IDR 12.69 quadrillion, with 50 issuers accounting for 75.70% of the total market cap, worth IDR 9.61 quadrillion. The top 10 issuers by market capitalization included Bank Central Asia (BBCA) and Bank Rakyat Indonesia (BREN), which exchanged positions between August and October 2024.
title: Indonesian Stock Exchange Sees 1.15% Month-to-Month Increase in Market Capitalization in October 2024

body: PT PP Tbk (PTPP) has secured new contracts worth IDR 24.4 trillion, or 76.25% of its 2024 target, with government projects contributing 41.24% and state-owned enterprises making up 30.76% of the total. The company's revenue and net profit also saw significant increases, with total revenue reaching IDR 17 trillion, up 14.54% year-on-year, and net profit rising 11.49% to IDR 267.28 billion.
title: PT PP Tbk Secures New Contracts Worth IDR 24.4 Trillion, 76.25% of 2024 Target.

body: PT TBS Energi Utama Tbk (TBS) will conduct a share buyback of up to 816.78 million shares, or 10% of its total issued and paid-up capital, at a price of IDR 520 per share, which will cost approximately IDR 425.29 billion. The company will use its internal cash for the buyback, which is not expected to affect its operational activities or financial capabilities, and will seek approval from its general meeting of shareholders scheduled for next Thursday.
title: PT TBS Energi Utama to Conduct Share Buyback of Up to 10% of Total Issued Capital.

body: PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) has announced an interim dividend distribution of IDR 50 per share, totaling IDR 6.16 trillion, with payment scheduled for December 11, 2024, following approval from the board of commissioners on November 8, 2024. The bank reported a net profit of IDR 41.07 trillion in the first nine months of 2024, with retained earnings of IDR 232.31 trillion and total equity of IDR 255.96 trillion as of September 30, 2024.
title: PT Bank Central Asia to Distribute IDR 6.16 Trillion in Interim Dividends to Shareholders.

body: PT Garuda Indonesia Tbk (GIAA) is projecting improved financial performance by the end of 2024, driven by strategies such as a new lease commercial term agreement using the ijarah scheme, which is expected to boost its financial foundation and improve equity. The company is also adding four new aircraft through global partnerships with Japan Airlines and Singapore Airlines, which will begin implementation in Q4 2024, further strengthening its presence in the global market.
title: Garuda Indonesia projects improved performance by end-2024 through production increase, global partnerships, and new lease agreements.

body: PT Buana Lintas Lautan Tbk (BULL) has postponed its plan to sell treasury shares obtained from a 2020 buyback due to the current stock price being below the average buyback price of IDR 296.60 per share, with the company setting a deadline of the 2025 annual general meeting to reassess. If the stock price remains low, the company may request shareholder approval to transfer treasury shares through capital reduction or other means, with the current stock price at IDR 114 per share, down 1.72% from the previous close.
title: Bull delays selling treasury shares due to low stock price, may seek shareholder approval for capital reduction if price doesn't improve by 2025.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) fell to its lowest point in three weeks, recording 7,190.83 at 10 am WIB, with other indices such as LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESG also experiencing declines. High-market-cap issuers like Bank Central Asia (BBCA), Bank Rakyat Indonesia (BBRI), Bank Mandiri (BMRI), Telkom Indonesia (TLKM), and Bank Negara Indonesia (BBNI) reported downturns, while some stocks like Mulia Boga Raya (MULI), Amman Mineral Internasional (AMIN), Bumi Resources Mineral (BRMS), Bumi Resources (BUMI), and Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia (TRIM) remained in the green zone.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Hits Three-Week Low Amid Broad Market Decline

body: PT Pantai Indah Kapuk Dua Tbk (PIKU) has seen its stock price surge 212.55% since its rights issue on August 19, 2024, and 987.37% since December 1, 2021, when PT Multi Artha Pratama initiated a tender offer, with its current market capitalization reaching IDR 283.64 trillion. The company's shares closed at IDR 16,800 per share last week, with a transaction volume of 6.09 million shares worth IDR 102.65 billion, compared to IDR 1,545 per share and IDR 417.15 million in transaction value in December 2021.
title: PT Pantai Indah Kapuk Dua's stock price surges 212.55% since rights issue and 987.37% since tender offer in 2021.

body: PT Aneka Tambang Tbk (ANTM) and PT Freeport Indonesia have signed a sale-purchase agreement, where Freeport will supply 30 tons of 99.99% pure gold per annum to ANTM, which will then be processed into precious metal products. This collaboration aims to increase gold downstreaming capacity and meet public demand for gold investment, with ANTM's precious metal gold products to be prioritized for sale in stock precious metal gold boutiques across Indonesia.
title: ANTAM and Freeport Indonesia Sign Agreement to Strengthen Domestic Gold Supply Chain

body: PT Sarana Multigriya Finansial (SMF) will issue a shelf-registration social bond worth IDR 1.2 trillion in Phase IV of 2024, with proceeds to be used to refinance housing residential financing and promote affordable housing projects for low-income households. The bond issuance, which starts on November 22, 2024, is fully committed and secured, with eight joint underwriters, including PT BCA Sekuritas, PT BNI Sekuritas, and PT Mandiri Sekuritas, each taking a 14.28% stake.
title: PT Sarana Multigriya Finansial to Issue IDR 1.2 Trillion Social Bond to Promote Affordable Housing.

body: PT Mandiri Tunas Finance, a subsidiary of PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI), is issuing a shelf-registration bond VI phase IV worth IDR 1.61 trillion, part of a public offering program targeting IDR 5 trillion in total proceeds. The bond offering, consisting of two series with coupons of 6.70% and 6.85% per annum, will be listed on the Indonesia Stock Exchange on November 20, 2024, with lead underwriters including PT BRI Danareksa Sekuritas, PT Mandiri Sekuritas, and PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk (TRIM).
title: PT Mandiri Tunas Finance Issues IDR 1.61 Trillion Shelf-Registration Bond in Phase IV of 2024 Program

body: Indonesia's foreign exchange reserves in October 2024 reached USD 151.2 billion, up from USD 149.9 billion in September 2024, equivalent to 6.6 months of import or 6.4 months of government foreign debt repayment. The increase in forex reserves was driven by tax income and foreign debt withdrawal, and Bank Indonesia believes the current reserves will maintain external sector resiliency, supporting positive export prospects and attractive investment returns.
title: Indonesia's foreign exchange reserves reach USD 151.2 billion in October 2024, exceeding international adequacy standards.
